chunks.heading: What does a "break in service" mean for my Fund participation?
chunks.text: A break in service does not necessarily mean the end of your participation period; it simply refers to an interruption in your contributory service. Your participation in the Fund pauses when you take a break from your employing organization.

chunks.heading: What must I decide upon separation if I intend to return to a UNJSPF member organization?
chunks.text: Upon separation, you must decide whether to make a benefit election or postpone this decision (deferment) if you intend to return to a UNJSPF member organization.

chunks.heading: What happens if I return to a UNJSPF member organization within 36 months of separation?
chunks.text: You may choose to defer your benefit election. If you rejoin a member organization during this time, your participation is considered continued. The gap itself is a break in service that cannot be made pensionable.

chunks.heading: What happens if my break in service exceeds 36 months?
chunks.text: You start a new period of participation with a new date of entry, and you must make a benefit election for the earlier period.

chunks.heading: What happens if I already received a payment from the Fund after separation?
chunks.text: If you already received a payout from the Fund after separation, that participation period has ended regardless of the length of the break.

chunks.heading: How can I track periods of break in service?
chunks.text: Periods of break in service are tracked in your annual pension statement, available via your Member Self-Service account.
